name: Girona Cathedral and Arab Baths old-town walk
summary: A dense, memorable old-town loop combining Girona Cathedral's monumental nave with the nearby medieval bath complex.
whyItMatters: Girona is listed as a non-viewing trip area, and this pairing gives a strong sense of the city without requiring a full museum day.
timeRequired: 90-150 minutes for cathedral plus baths; 45-60 minutes if exterior-only.
ticketOrReservationNeed: Tickets likely needed for interiors; check each official site.
pros: Very high cultural density in a small walkable area
Works as a flexible Girona morning or late-afternoon stop
Cathedral and baths are close enough to combine without complex transit
cons: Old-town stairs and July heat may slow the group
Opening hours vary by monument and religious services

logistics.opening_hours: Verify Cathedral and Banys Arabs hours on official pages for the exact visit date.
logistics.reservation_needed: Recommended for cathedral interior if timing is tight.
logistics.distance_from_base: Girona base dependent; both are in the Barri Vell / old town.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not a race viewing point; useful before the Stage 3 Pyrenees transfer.
logistics.transit: Walk from central Girona; taxi useful if lodging is outside the old town.
logistics.parking: Use perimeter parking; old-town driving is not a good assumption.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No TDF closure assumed; verify any local events.

timing.best_visit_window: Girona non-race morning or evening before departing toward Puigcerda.
timing.race_day_risk: Low for Girona itself, but transfer timing to Puigcerda matters.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with coffee or lunch in the Barri Vell before driving toward the Pyrenees.
